commonIssues:
A major issue for laborers, elderly, and those working in agriculture is that fingerprints often do not match the digital records, leading to denial of rations.

How to fix:

  • Under Food and Civil Supplies directives, request the shopkeeper to verify your identity via OTP sent to your Aadhaar-linked mobile phone.
  • If that fails, visit the local E-Mitra center and undergo a biometric data update on your Aadhaar card so it matches effectively.

Mandatory Aadhaar linking has caused many, particularly in rural areas, to be excluded due to linking errors. In June 2024, it was reported that children and elderly citizens faced difficulties with E-KYC due to fingerprint or iris scan mismatches.

How to fix:

  • Visit an E-Mitra center with the physical ration card and Aadhaar copies of mismatched family members to submit a forced re-seeding request.
  • For elderly citizens or bed-ridden individuals with wiped prints, a manual exception can be granted by writing directly to the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM).

Due to errors in the IMPDS system, many poor families have been incorrectly listed as high-income earners, car owners, or income tax payers, resulting in them being wrongly removed from the free ration scheme.

How to fix:

  • Gather an updated Income Certificate and an affidavit stating no vehicle or tax is owned. Upload these proofs through an E-Mitra portal seeking a correction in status.
  • Use the "Rajasthan Sampark" portal (contact toll-free 181) to escalate cases where you are wrongly accused of owning expensive assets.

Systemic errors in data entry have resulted in absurd age recordings (e.g., children listed as over 100 years old), making beneficiaries ineligible.

How to fix:

  • Utilize the Jan Aadhaar system—ensure that the Jan Aadhaar card, which is closely linked to Ration Cards in Rajasthan, has entirely correct data. Updating the age on Jan Aadhaar through E-Mitra will sync to the Food Department data.
  • Provide valid proof of age like birth certificate, school leaving certificate, or Aadhaar.

The voluntary "Give Up" campaign aimed at removing ineligible beneficiaries has caused anxiety and confusion, with some eligible individuals mistakenly receiving notices.

How to fix:

  • If you receive a notice by mistake, do not ignore it. Re-validate your eligibility by submitting a signed declaration to the Block Development Officer (BDO) with your current income certificate.
  • Do not click any unverified links claiming you are being removed—respond only via formal government channels or at the local Panchayat.

Many struggle with accessing online services, facing issues with E-Mitra centers, and are forced to make long, costly trips to district offices to rectify errors.

How to fix:

  • Avoid unofficial centers. There is an official "E-Mitra App" developed by DoIT&C, Rajasthan. Use it to securely perform minor corrections directly from your smartphone.
  • If an E-Mitra retailer overcharges you for a Ration Card query, note down their kiosk ID and file a complaint via the unified Rajasthan Sampark center.

In certain cases, new rules have restricted unmarried individuals from obtaining separate ration cards if they live with their parents, often creating problems for those who have separated.

How to fix:

  • Submit utility bills (electricity or water) in the unmarried individual's name showing a different floor or separate residential portion to prove a distinct "household" status.
  • An endorsement letter from the local Sarpanch or Ward Councilor confirming physical separation is highly effective at the Tahsildar office.
